name: Raspberry Almond Layer Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter and let it cool.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, beat the egg yolks. Blend in the apple juice concentrate, butter, and almond extract.
4. In another bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add to the egg yolk mixture, beating until well-blended. Stir in the chopped almonds.
5. In a small bowl, beat the egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff peaks form. Gently fold into the batter and spread evenly into the prepared pans.
6. Bake for 18-20 minutes or until golden brown around the edges. Cool in the pans on wire racks for 10 minutes, then turn onto racks to cool completely.
7. In a small bowl, beat the heavy cream until soft peaks form. Brush raspberry liqueur evenly over the cake layers and stack them together.
8. Frost the top and sides with the whipped cream mixture and press slivered almonds around the edge. Garnish with fresh raspberries if desired.
Nutrition information per slice: 595 calories, 10 gm protein, 59 gm carbohydrate, 36 gm fat, 167 mg cholesterol, 402 mg sodium, 2 diabetic starch/bread exchange, 7 diabetic fat exchange, 1-3 diabetic fruit exchange.
